summary:
Claim: Complete perineal tear is the least common complication in outlet forceps delivery.  
Evidence: - Full-thickness perineal tears (<5%) are rare due to anatomical resistance and lack of direct mechanical pathway. - Compared to perineal lacerations (up to 50%) and vulval hematomas (8–14%), their incidence is substantially lower.  
Answer: A
